qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: DoS via Unbounded Image Fetching Without Size Limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: Denial of Service (DoS) - Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets SurveyJS image URL fetching and unbounded ArrayBuffer allocation, not the advisory's jsPDF Node local file inclusion/path traversal through filesystem-path arguments.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via Image URL Fetching Without Validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets arbitrary server-side HTTP image URL fetching in SurveyJS, while the advisory targets jsPDF Node local filesystem reads via loadFile/addImage/html/addFont path handling.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XSS via HTML Question in SurveyJS PDF Generator
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets browser DOM insertion and HTML sanitization bypasses, not the advisory's jsPDF Node local file inclusion/path traversal behavior.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described jsPDF local file inclusion/path traversal root cause. The advisory is about survey-pdf depending on affected jsPDF versions where user-controlled filesystem paths to loadFile and related Node-build methods can be included in generated PDFs; the final findings instead cover SurveyJS image URL fetching DoS, SurveyJS image URL SSRF, and browser HTML/XSS rendering.
evidence: GHSA-h3q6-jfrg-3x6q states survey-pdf is exposed through jsPDF <=3.0.4 Local File Inclusion/Path Traversal and is fixed by upgrading jsPDF to >=4.0.0 in survey-pdf v1.12.59/v2.5.5.

The parent jsPDF advisory GHSA-f8cm-6447-x5h2 describes user control of the first argument to loadFile and related addImage/html/addFont methods in the Node build causing arbitrary local file contents to be included in PDFs.
dos_via_unbounded_image_fetch reports src/utils/image/node.ts fetch(url) and res.arrayBuffer() without size or timeout limits.
ssrf_via_image_url reports src/utils/image/node.ts fetch(url) without URL validation or private-address blocking.
xss_via_html_question reports src/helper_survey.ts insertAdjacentHTML and src/jspdf_plugins/from_html.js document.writeln sanitization issues.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1/Tier2/Tier3 runtime evaluation is not applicable.
